title: Asukas Appearance
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's Appearance             Quote Post by Dave » Tue Aug 31, 2004 10:08 pm Is Asuka suppose to appear more caucasian than Japanese? To me the design of her face does seem a little bit different from the Japanese characters in the show. However it is nearly impossible to tell the difference in caucasian and Japanese characters when it comes to anime (there are a few styles where the characters are notably Japanese, but these are few and far between), so I suppose this could just be a little wishful thinking on my part.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Tue Aug 31, 2004 10:27 pm All of the characters have little traits about their design that are different than the others. I'm not sure if she's actually meant to look more caucasian. But she is over German descent to some degree... altho, they have the red hair/blue eyes for that.

[]:             Quote Post by houtaru » Wed Sep 01, 2004 2:54 am I wondered about that myself. I always thought she was german looking. She's supposed to be half Japanese. So I guess she's both german and Japanese. Did she grow up in America or Germany in the series?

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Wed Sep 01, 2004 9:11 am I think Germany, since she was speaking it when she first arrived. The manga just says she's an American citizen.

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Wed Sep 01, 2004 9:55 am If you haven't noticed...but all anime characters tend to look more cuasian than Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 01, 2004 10:44 am From about the 20s-50s comic artists and animators were heavily influenced by the Disney animation studio. One manga artist named Osamu Tezuka, known for works such as Astro Boy and Metropolis, was especially known for being influenced by Disney. In fact, in many people's opinion, Tenzuka is the "father" of the modern manga and anime style. The anime/manga style of today is infact an extreme abstraction on Disney. This is why characters have a caucasion appearance in many instances. Asuka is 3/4 German, 1/4 Japanese, but with American nationality, according to the End of Evangelion program book. So in terms of ethnicity, she is predminantly caucasion. When dealing with character designs, I think its fairly easy to just mix and match. I think the only thing about Asuka's appearance that is not stereotypically Japanese is the brilliant red hair and blue eyes. Natural red heads are uncommon in Japan, and those with that kind of natural red hair are extremely rare. Most people with hair of that color are fake red heads. I'm not sure about blue eyes, but I believe thats quite uncommon to be natural, so I question Shinji's blue eyes as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 01, 2004 11:18 am The Eva Monkey wrote: From about the 20s-50s comic artists and animators were heavily influenced by the Disney animation studio. One manga artist named Osamu Tezuka, known for works such as Astro Boy and Metropolis, was especially known for being influenced by Disney. In fact, in many people's opinion, Tenzuka is the "father" of the modern manga and anime style. The anime/manga style of today is infact an extreme abstraction on Disney. This is why characters have a caucasion appearance in many instances. Asuka is 3/4 German, 1/4 Japanese, but with American nationality, according to the End of Evangelion program book. So in terms of ethnicity, she is predminantly caucasion. When dealing with character designs, I think its fairly easy to just mix and match. I think the only thing about Asuka's appearance that is not stereotypically Japanese is the brilliant red hair and blue eyes. Natural red heads are uncommon in Japan, and those with that kind of natural red hair are extremely rare. Most people with hair of that color are fake red heads. I'm not sure about blue eyes, but I believe thats quite uncommon to be natural, so I question Shinji's blue eyes as well.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 01, 2004 12:56 pm Dave wrote: I already knew about Osamu and his love for Disney.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Sep 01, 2004 4:31 pm Your'e not reaching at all EM. The whole time I watched the Nadia series I could see parallels. Personally I think Jean is Kensuke's clone sent back in time to live in France.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Wed Sep 01, 2004 6:41 pm Dave wrote: I already knew about Osamu and his love for Disney. I was wondering if anyone knew what Gainax imagined Asuka would look like when they created her.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 01, 2004 6:55 pm Asuka Langley wrote: Alright, but don't you think that she is designed in the way that they imagined her?

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Wed Sep 01, 2004 7:05 pm I did not misinterpret. You asked how they imagined her, so dont you think the artist would try to make that character look as close to the originally 'imagined' idea as possible? In order to find out how they 'imagined her' you'd probably have to ask Sadamoto himself. And he'd probably say something like 'cute' or whatever.

[]: would it?             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Tue Sep 14, 2004 4:58 pm i wonder if that would be possible

[]: Re: would it?             Quote Post by advent_child » Wed Sep 22, 2004 3:31 pm Dark FireStar wrote: i wonder if that would be possible

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 03, 2004 2:19 am Asuka Langley wrote: I mean, earlier sketches show her with shorter, more brownish hair and freckles.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Sun Oct 03, 2004 12:24 pm In the manga character biographies, and I'm not sure about the ones on the DVDs, it says that Asuka is an American citizen. Whether or not she briefly mentioned this, slips my mind currently.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sun Oct 03, 2004 1:04 pm who knows...maybe had something to do with eva?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 03, 2004 6:57 pm Asuka Langley wrote: In the manga character biographies, and I'm not sure about the ones on the DVDs, it says that Asuka is an American citizen. Whether or not she briefly mentioned this, slips my mind currently.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Mon Oct 11, 2004 4:13 pm I thinks shes just a white girl so just leave it at that lol

[]:             Quote Post by advent_child » Sat Oct 23, 2004 4:15 pm Dark FireStar wrote: I thinks shes just a white girl so just leave it at that lol
